root_cause.text: The vulnerability is caused by the use of Spring Expression Language (SpEL) to render the default whitelabel approval and error views (`SpelView`), allowing attacker-controlled input (such as scopes or client IDs) to be evaluated as executable SpEL expressions.
root_cause.evidence: The patch deletes `SpelView.java` (which used `SpelExpressionParser` and `StandardEvaluationContext`) and updates `WhitelabelApprovalEndpoint` and `WhitelabelErrorEndpoint` to construct HTML manually using `StringBuilder` while properly escaping inputs with `HtmlUtils.htmlEscape()`. The test `testApprovalPageWithSuspectScope` explicitly verifies that a malicious SpEL scope `"${T(java.lang.Runtime).getRuntime().exec(\"cd ..\")}"` is properly escaped instead of executed.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The vulnerability is caused by the use of Spring Expression Language (SpEL) to render the default whitelabel approval and error views (`SpelView`), allowing attacker-controlled input (such as scopes or client IDs) to be evaluated as executable SpEL expressions.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch deletes `SpelView.java` (which used `SpelExpressionParser` and `StandardEvaluationContext`) and updates `WhitelabelApprovalEndpoint` and `WhitelabelErrorEndpoint` to construct HTML manually using `StringBuilder` while properly escaping inputs with `HtmlUtils.htmlEscape()`. The test `testApprovalPageWithSuspectScope` explicitly verifies that a malicious SpEL scope `"${T(java.lang.Runtime).getRuntime().exec(\"cd ..\")}"` is properly escaped instead of executed.

intent_record.intent_summary: Delete the vulnerable SpelView class to prevent potential SpEL injection vulnerabilities.
intent_record.what: The entire SpelView.java file was deleted.
intent_record.how: Removes the SpelView class, preventing its use for rendering templates via Spring Expression Language.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update imports to support enhanced CSRF and view handling capabilities in the approval controller.
intent_record.what: Reorganized existing imports and added new ones including CsrfToken, View, ServletUriComponentsBuilder, HtmlUtils, and HttpServletResponse.
intent_record.how: Provides the necessary class dependencies for the controller to handle CSRF tokens, HTML escaping, and view rendering for the authorization approval page.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove SpEL evaluation and add HTML escaping to fix a potential Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability.
intent_record.what: Replaced the SpelView-based template rendering with a custom View implementation and manual StringBuilder-based HTML construction in createTemplate, while also adding HtmlUtils.htmlEscape to sanitize inputs like clientId, csrfToken parameters, and scopes.
intent_record.how: The change removes the dependency on Spring Expression Language (SpEL) for rendering the OAuth approval page, manually constructing the HTML strings instead. By using HtmlUtils.htmlEscape, it prevents Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities that could occur if malicious user input was injected into the client ID or scope parameters.

intent_record.intent_summary: Reorganize and add necessary imports for View and HttpServletResponse.
intent_record.what: Reordered existing imports and added new imports for View and HttpServletResponse.
intent_record.how: Makes the View and HttpServletResponse classes available for use within the controller.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update error template placeholder syntax to prevent unintended expression evaluation.
intent_record.what: Changed the template placeholder syntax from ${errorSummary} to %errorSummary% in the ERROR string constant.
intent_record.how: Alters the template string to prevent unintended expression evaluation during error rendering.

intent_record.intent_summary: Replace SpelView with a standard View to prevent Server-Side Template Injection vulnerabilities in the error endpoint.
intent_record.what: Replaced the use of SpelView with an anonymous View implementation that directly writes string-replaced error content to the response.
intent_record.how: Prevents the error summary from being evaluated as a Spring Expression Language (SpEL) expression, thereby eliminating a potential template injection vulnerability.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove SpelView tests to clean up the test suite.
intent_record.what: Deleted the entire SpelViewTests.java file containing unit tests.
intent_record.how: Removes the test cases for SpelView from the test suite, likely because the underlying feature or class was removed or heavily refactored.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove unused imports to clean up the code.
intent_record.what: Removed unused imports from the test file.
intent_record.how: This change has no impact on runtime behavior as it only removes unused dependencies.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add necessary imports for collections and assertions.
intent_record.what: Added imports for Java Collections and JUnit assertions.
intent_record.how: Provides necessary dependencies for the class.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add unit tests to verify proper escaping of malicious scopes and correct form rendering in the whitelabel approval endpoint.
intent_record.what: Added three new unit test methods to WhitelabelApprovalEndpointTests to verify the rendering of the OAuth approval page under different conditions.
intent_record.how: Increases test coverage by ensuring that malicious Spring Expression Language (SpEL) scopes are properly escaped, and that approval forms render correctly both with and without specific scopes and CSRF tokens.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update HTML attribute assertions to expect double quotes instead of single quotes.
intent_record.what: Changed single quotes to double quotes in string assertions.
intent_record.how: Updates the test to look for HTML attributes enclosed in double quotes, matching the updated page rendering.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update test assertions to expect double quotes in HTML attributes.
intent_record.what: Changed single quotes to double quotes in the HTML attribute strings used in the test assertions.
intent_record.how: Ensures the test assertions correctly match the generated HTML output which now uses double quotes for attributes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update HTML assertions to expect double quotes instead of single quotes.
intent_record.what: Changed single quotes to double quotes in the string arguments of assertTrue statements within the verifyAuthorizationPage method.
intent_record.how: Updates the test to correctly assert against the generated HTML, which now uses double quotes instead of single quotes for its attributes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update authorization page test assertions to expect double quotes for HTML attributes.
intent_record.what: Single quotes were replaced with double quotes in HTML attribute string assertions.
intent_record.how: Updates the test assertions to match the updated HTML rendering which now uses double quotes, preventing false test failures.

grouping_result.intent: Delete the vulnerable SpelView class to prevent potential SpEL injection vulnerabilities.
Reorganize and add necessary imports for View and HttpServletResponse.
Replace SpelView with a standard View to prevent Server-Side Template Injection vulnerabilities in the error endpoint.
Remove SpelView tests to clean up the test suite.
Remove unused imports to clean up the code.
Add necessary imports for collections and assertions.
Add unit tests to verify proper escaping of malicious scopes and correct form rendering in the whitelabel approval endpoint.
Update authorization page test assertions to expect double quotes for HTML attributes.

consistency_review.core_intent: Update and reorganize imports to support view handling and CSRF capabilities in the controller.
Resolve expression evaluation and template injection vulnerabilities in the error handling endpoint.
Update test assertions to expect double quotes instead of single quotes for HTML attributes.

relevance_review.reason: Deleting the vulnerable SpelView class directly eliminates the source of the SpEL injection vulnerability described in the root cause.
The group only consists of import updates and reorganization, which does not directly address the SpEL injection vulnerability.
The group directly addresses the vulnerability by replacing the vulnerable SpelView with a standard View and removing SpEL evaluation from the error handling templates.
Removing SpelView tests is a direct consequence of addressing the vulnerability, which involved removing or replacing the vulnerable SpelView component.
Removing unused imports is a code cleanup task and does not address the SpEL injection vulnerability in the whitelabel views.
Adding imports for collections and assertions is a general code maintenance task and does not directly address the SpEL injection vulnerability.
The group adds unit tests to verify the proper escaping of malicious input in the whitelabel approval endpoint, directly testing the fix for the vulnerability root cause.
The changes only update HTML formatting assertions in tests (expecting double quotes instead of single quotes) and do not directly address or reflect the SpEL injection vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This line returns a new SpelView populated with attacker-controlled scopes or client IDs without proper escaping, triggering the SpEL injection.
This line renders the error template using SpelView, exposing the error endpoint to SpEL injection.
This line in SpelView parses SpEL expressions, where the vulnerability originates from evaluating untrusted input as code.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement instantiates a SpelView using the approval template, which evaluates attacker-controlled input as SpEL expressions, leading to the vulnerability.
This statement instantiates a SpelView using the error template, which is similarly vulnerable to SpEL injection when rendering error information.
This is the core parsing logic in SpelView that evaluates the untrusted template strings as Spring Expression Language expressions.

If user defines a @Bean of type ApprovalStore the Authoriza
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 787c9b47d801b0cf08d35bca4805986c43f56836
Author: Dave Syer <dsyer@gopivotal.com>
Date:   Mon Mar 17 16:14:03 2014 +0000

    Add support for ApprovalStore in @EnableAuthorizationServer
    
    If user defines a @Bean of type ApprovalStore the AuthorizationEndpoint and
    WhitelabelApprovalEndpoint change their behaviour accordingly.

diff --git a/spring-security-oauth2/src/main/java/org/springframework/security/oauth2/provider/endpoint/WhitelabelApprovalEndpoint.java b/spring-security-o
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces logic in `createScopes` where user-controlled `scope` strings are directly injected into the `template` via `.replace("%scope%", scope)`. This template is then passed to `new SpelView(template)`, meaning any SpEL expressions within the user-controlled input will be evaluated, leading to the vulnerability.

diff --git a/spring-security-oauth2/src/main/java/org/springframework/security/oauth2/

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 64f8ea24e3c3b125ac97ebc7f753255c50e34460
Author: Dave Syer <dsyer@vmware.com>
Date:   Thu Jun 7 12:11:35 2012 +0100

    SECOAUTH-273: add Whitelabel endpoints and @FrameworkEndpoint

diff --git a/spring-security-oauth2/src/main/java/org/springframework/security/oauth2/provider/endpoint/WhitelabelApprovalEndpoint.java b/spring-security-oauth2/src/main/java/org/springframework/security/oauth2/provider/endpoint/WhitelabelApprovalEndpoint.java
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..b436f43f
--
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces the `SpelView` class which evaluates template placeholders using Spring Expression Language (SpEL) via `parser.parseExpression(name)`. It uses `PropertyPlaceholderHelper.replacePlaceholders`, which recursively evaluates placeholders in the resolved values by default. This enables SpEL injection if attacker-controlled input (like clientId or error summary) is returned and contains `${...}`.

Author: Dave Syer <dsyer@gopivotal.com>

diff --git a/spring-security-oauth2/src/main/java/org/springframework/security/oauth2/provider/endpoint/SpelView.java b/spring-security-oauth2/src/main/jav
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces `SpelView` which parses placeholders in the template as SpEL expressions (`parser.parseExpression(name).getValue(context)`) using `StandardEvaluationContext`. This evaluates attacker-controlled input as executable SpEL, matching the vulnerability root cause. There is no sanitization or use of a safe evaluation context (like `SimpleEvaluationContext`), so the fix is not present.
